Our Approach

Fellowship rooted in community

Urafiki meaning 'friendship' in Swahili is our philosophy. Lasting change grows from genuine human connection, trust, and shared purpose.

Education Access

Removing barriers to quality education for youth in conflict-affected communities.

Voice & Expression

Poetry competitions and writing programs that amplify youth perspectives.

Agricultural Training

Practical land-based entrepreneurship through Shule ya Ardhi for youth aged 16–28.

Girls, Women & GBV Survivors

Education, healing, and economic independence for girls, women, and GBV survivors.

A Message from Our Founder

"I did not choose this cause.
This cause chose me."

The girls of eastern DRC did not choose to be born into conflict. GBV survivors did not choose the violence done to them. But we can choose to respond with education, with dignity, with economic opportunity, and with trust in women's ability to lead.

Daniel Rukwasha
Founder & Executive Director
Berea College, Kentucky · Originally from Goma, DRC
Our Story

Born from a belief that every child deserves a future

Founded by a young leader from eastern DRC, Urafiki Fellowship exists to give the next generation what circumstances denied to many.

Daniel Rukwasha

Founder & Executive Director

Originally from eastern DRC, Daniel founded Urafiki Fellowship while studying Computer Science & Quantitative Economics at Berea College, Kentucky holding a full-tuition merit scholarship. His work is rooted in a deep personal commitment to Goma and North Kivu his home. He speaks four languages and believes education, economic independence, and peacebuilding are inseparable.

🇫🇷 French 🇬🇧 English 🇨🇩 Swahili 🇨🇩 Lingala
Who We Are

Our mission & values

Urafiki Fellowship is a humanitarian education nonprofit dedicated to youth empowerment, peacebuilding, and women's dignity in eastern DRC one of the world's most fragile regions.

We believe sustainable peace begins with empowered young people equipped with knowledge, skills, voice, and networks.

  • Youth-centered design: programs built with and for young people
  • Community accountability: local partners co-own every initiative
  • Long-term thinking: we invest in sustainable change
  • Dignity above all: every young person deserves full respect
Leadership

Our team

What We Do

Our Programs

Four interconnected initiatives tackling education, livelihoods, peace, and women's empowerment in eastern DRC.

01
Education

FESCOL GOMA Festival Scolaire de Goma

A city-wide nine-discipline school championship celebrating academic and cultural achievement in Goma. Creates healthy competition and provides a platform for young people to shine.

Goma, North Kivu9 DisciplinesSecondary Schools
02
Peacebuilding

Irenge Rukwasha Peace Prize

Annual poetry competition across Goma schools amplifying youth voices for peace, reconciliation, and dignity.

AnnualPoetryGoma
03
Livelihoods

Shule ya Ardhi School of the Earth

Eight-month agricultural training and entrepreneurship for youth 16–28. Four phases: foundations, applied skills, enterprise development, community practice.

Ages 16–288 Months4 Phases
04
Women's Empowerment

For Girls, Women & GBV Survivors

Eastern DRC has endured one of the world's most severe humanitarian crises. Girls and women bear the heaviest burden. Our model walks with women from crisis to classroom to marketplace, building skills, confidence, and financial independence.

Seeking $50,000–$60,000 Seed Funding · FY 2026–2027

Restoring Education, Dignity & Independence

When girls are educated, GBV survivors are healed, and women are economically independent communities build resilience to resist violence, exploitation, and cycles of poverty. No woman is abandoned midway.

<20%
Girls complete primary school in conflict zones
#1
DRC most dangerous for women (UN)
7.2M
Internally displaced (UNHCR 2024)

Safety & Healing

Safe spaces, GBV support, trauma-informed care

Girls' Education

Full scholarships for 60 girls aged 8–18

Women as Teachers

15 women certified as teachers & leaders

Economic Empowerment

Vocational training + micro-grants for 25 women

Mobile Learning

Reaching 30 displaced families in camps

Revolving Fund

Self-sustaining community fund by Year 3

North KivuIturiGirls 8–18GBV Survivors$50K–$60K Seed

Voices from the Field

Stories of change

"

Before FESCOL, I never thought I could compete. Winning the mathematics award showed me that talent has no address.

FESCOL GOMA participant, age 17
"

The Peace Prize gave me a place to put my grief into words. I wrote about losing my brother. People listened.

Irenge Rukwasha laureate, age 16
"

Shule ya Ardhi taught me that the land I grew up on is not just earth it is opportunity.

Shule ya Ardhi graduate, age 22
"

Urafiki treated the students like they were already the leaders they could become. That attitude changes everything.

Headteacher, Goma
Our Journey

How we got here

2022
Urafiki Fellowship founded
Established to bridge the opportunity gap for youth in eastern DRC.
2023
Irenge Rukwasha Peace Prize launched
First poetry competition across Goma, hundreds of participants.
2024
FESCOL GOMA launched
City-wide school championship, 12+ partner schools.
2025–2026
Shule ya Ardhi & GBV Program launched
Agricultural training + women's empowerment. Seeking $50K–$60K seed funding.

Urafiki Ambassador Program

Become a Voice for Eastern DRC

Ambassadors carry Urafiki Fellowship's message into the world. You don't need to be in DRC to make a difference your voice and network matter wherever you are.

  • Official Urafiki Fellowship Ambassador certificate
  • Access to program materials and impact reports
  • Regular updates and briefings from the Founder
  • Listed on our website as a community advocate
Apply to Be an Ambassador →
Support the Mission

Your gift changes lives

100% of donations go directly to youth programs in eastern DRC. Every dollar matters.

Why give to Urafiki?

Every dollar goes directly to youth programs school championships, peace ceremonies, agricultural training, and support for girls and GBV survivors in North Kivu. No overhead. Just impact.
